title: Football Supporters Europe & Euroconsumers File EU Commission Complaint Against FIFA Over Ticket Pricing
detail: Two major European football supporter and consumer rights organizations — Football Supporters Europe (FSE) and Euroconsumers — filed a formal complaint with the European Commission on April 3 alleging FIFA abused its dominant market position to impose excessive and exploitative ticket prices for the 2026 World Cup. The complaint comes as Final ticket face values reach $10,990 (up from $8,680 in prior phases), with secondary prices hitting $143,750 on FIFA's own official resale platform — 41 times face value. The organizations argued that FIFA's 500-million-application demand demonstrates market dominance being exploited in violation of EU competition law. FSE had previously described prices as 'up to seven times higher than Qatar 2022.' The complaint follows a formal letter from US Congress members calling the tournament 'the most financially exclusionary ever,' and comes one day after the April 2 ticket website glitch that left thousands of fans stranded in the wrong queue for 90+ minutes. FIFA has not publicly responded to the EU complaint.

title: Iran-FIFA Talks Progressing — Tucson Training Base Confirmed, FIFA Congress Decision Due April 30
detail: Al Jazeera and ESPN reported on April 2–3 that Iran-FIFA talks on World Cup participation are progressing following Gianni Infantino's meeting with Iranian football federation officials in Antalya, Turkey. Infantino promised 'the best possible conditions' for Iran's participation, and Iran's football federation representatives confirmed the team is planning to arrive at Kino Sports Complex in Tucson, Arizona by June 10 for Group G preparations. Iran's three group matches remain scheduled at US venues: vs New Zealand (SoFi Stadium, LA — June 15), vs Belgium (SoFi Stadium, LA — June 21), vs Egypt (Lumen Field, Seattle — June 26). Despite this diplomatic progress, Iran's Sports Minister Ahmad Donyamali maintained the government's public position that Iran 'under no circumstances' can participate due to ongoing US-Iran military conflict. The formal decision point remains the FIFA Congress in Vancouver on April 30, 2026 — 27 days away — where contingency planning for an AFC replacement must begin if the standoff continues. The team is in a politically unresolved situation: football federation willing, government opposed, FIFA committed to the existing schedule.

title: USMNT Post-Camp Analysis: Pulisic in 8-Match Scoring Drought, Roster Questions Loom for Pochettino
detail: ESPN's USMNT World Cup Big Board 6.0, published in early April following the March camp losses to Belgium and Portugal, highlighted a critical concern: captain Christian Pulisic had gone 8 consecutive matches without a goal across club (AC Milan) and country, with 0 goals in all of 2026 for either side. Pochettino's 4-3-3 system conceded 7 goals across two friendlies (2–5 to Belgium, 0–2 to Portugal) and drew renewed calls to adjust the defensive midfield pairing. Key questions ahead of the May 26 roster announcement: whether Ricardo Pepi can hold a starting striker role, who partners Tyler Adams in midfield, and whether the back four exposed by Portugal's transition play will be restructured. The USMNT's next fixture is May 31 vs Senegal in Charlotte — Pochettino's only remaining test before naming his 26-man World Cup squad. Group D opponents Kosovo, Paraguay, and Australia will all have studied the Belgium and Portugal footage.
